content.problemStatement:
Soma is a fashionable girl. She absolutely loves shiny stones that she can put on as jewellery accessories. She has been collecting stones since her childhood - now she has become really good with identifying which ones are fake and which ones are not.  Her King requested for her help in mining precious stones, so she has told him which all stones are jewels and which are not. Given her description, your task is  to count the number of jewel stones. 


More formally, you're given a string J composed of latin characters where each character is a jewel. You're also given a string S composed of latin characters where each character is a mined stone. You have to find out how many characters of S are in J as well.
content.inputFormat: First line contains T, the number of test cases. Each test case consists of two lines: the first line is a string J (the set of jewel types) and the second line is a string S (the mined stones).
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output a single integer on its own line – the number of characters in S that also appear in J.

sampleTestCases.input:
4
abc
abcdef
aA
abAZ
aaa
a
what
none
sampleTestCases.explanation:
Test case 1: jewels {a,b,c}, stones abcdef → 3 matches.
Test case 2: jewels {a,A}, stones abAZ → a and A match → 2.
Test case 3: jewels {a}, stones a → 1.
Test case 4: jewels {w,h,a,t}, stones none → 0.
All six stones are the jewel type 'z'.
Every character in the stone string appears in the jewel string, so the count is 6.

abc
abcdef

abc

solution.approach: The problem can be solved by iterating over each character in the string S and checking if it exists in the string J. This can be done efficiently by converting the string J into a set for O(1) lookup time. The time complexity of this approach is O(T * (|J| + |S|)) where T is the number of test cases, |J| is the length of the string J, and |S| is the length of the string S.

code.Python3:
import sys

def main():
    data = sys.stdin.read().split()
    if not data:
        return
    T = int(data[0])
    idx = 1
    out = []
    for _ in range(T):
        J = data[idx]; S = data[idx + 1]; idx += 2
        jewels = set(J)
        cnt = sum(1 for ch in S if ch in jewels)
        out.append(str(cnt))
    sys.stdout.write('\n'.join(out))

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
